evals.prompt: how do i actually get customers for my thing? it's PixelPatch, a $9/month browser extension that auto-fixes broken images and layout bugs on your own website. positioning is basically: for indie web devs who hate manual QA, it catches visual regressions automatically. mostly solo devs and tiny agencies.
evals.expected_output: A go-to-market plan for a low-priced, self-serve small-business/PLG product that picks product-led as the dominant motion, ranks channels with concrete first tests, recommends transparent monthly pricing, maps the funnel with leak points, sequences a launch, and gives a 90-day action list.
assertions: Names product-led/self-serve as the dominant motion and justifies it using the actual numbers in the prompt — that $9/month (roughly $108/year) cannot fund any human sales touch — rather than asserting PLG as a generic default for developer tools

Names specific places indie devs and tiny agencies already are (e.g. Hacker News, dev-focused subreddits, Product Hunt, dev newsletters, npm/GitHub adjacency, long-tail SEO on 'visual regression testing') instead of listing generic channel categories like 'content marketing', 'paid social', and 'partnerships'

Recommends starting with only one or two channels, and for each of those gives all three of: a stated hypothesis, a concrete first test that could be run this month, and a named success signal with a number or threshold attached

Rules out or heavily qualifies paid acquisition on CAC-payback grounds specific to a $9/month price — i.e. shows or states that allowable CAC is on the order of tens of dollars, so paid channels only work at a very low CPA or with an annual plan to pull cash forward

Picks a value metric a solo dev or tiny agency already counts — sites/domains monitored, pages scanned, or checks run — and explicitly reasons about why per-seat pricing is a poor fit when the typical customer is one person

Identifies at most two leakiest funnel stages and one of them is specific to a browser extension's activation problem: the gap between install and the user actually connecting a real site and seeing a first regression caught, rather than declaring every stage equally risky

Ends with a 90-day action list where each item names the specific metric it moves (e.g. install-to-activation rate, trial-to-paid conversion, weekly signups from channel X) rather than a to-do list with no measurement attached

evals.prompt: Build a GTM plan for Rampart, a vendor-risk-management platform we sell to enterprise security and compliance teams at banks and insurers. ACV target is ~$120k/year, sales cycles run 4-6 months with procurement and a SOC 2 / security review, and the buying committee is a CISO, a compliance lead, and procurement. Positioning and ICP are already done. Assume annual contracts.
evals.expected_output: A GTM plan for a high-ACV enterprise product that chooses a sales-led/ABM motion with pilots and procurement, ranks enterprise-appropriate channels, recommends negotiated annual pricing anchored to value, maps a long funnel with leak points, and sequences a phased design-partner-to-GA launch.
assertions: Chooses a sales-led or ABM motion and justifies it specifically from the ~$120k ACV, the three-person buying committee, and the 4-6 month cycle — and explicitly rules out product-led self-serve rather than leaving it unaddressed
Treats the SOC 2 / security review and procurement as their own named funnel stages with their own exit criteria, not as a single lump 'legal and paperwork' step at the end

Assigns distinct roles to the three named stakeholders — CISO, compliance lead, procurement — describing what each one has to be convinced of and which stage they gate, rather than referring to them collectively as 'the buying committee'

Names at most two leakiest stages and picks ones specific to this deal shape (e.g. champion-to-committee handoff, or the security-review-to-procurement stretch where deals stall) instead of asserting that the whole cycle is risky

Prices against a value metric a bank already counts — number of vendors assessed/managed, or assessments per year — and recommends a published or internal list price to anchor negotiation plus a discount floor, rather than only saying 'negotiated, value-based, annual'

Recommends testing only one or two channels first, each with a hypothesis, a first test, and a success signal, and the channels are enterprise-appropriate (named-account ABM, analyst relations, CISO peer networks, compliance community events) rather than SEO, self-serve trials, or Product Hunt

The 90-day action list uses leading indicators consistent with a 4-6 month cycle — qualified target accounts, first meetings, design partners or pilots signed, a completed security questionnaire and SOC 2 evidence pack — and does not promise closed-won ARR inside the 90 days

evals.prompt: Can you put together a go-to-market plan for Bloomwrite, our AI writing assistant for mid-market marketing teams? Quick heads up: we don't really have positioning written down yet, we just have a rough idea of what it does.
evals.expected_output: The skill should recognize that positioning and ICP are missing and either run/insist on the positioning skill first or explicitly establish positioning before proceeding, then deliver a hybrid mid-market GTM plan with land-and-expand packaging.
assertions: Flags the missing positioning and ICP as a blocking prerequisite before any motion or channel work — either routing to the positioning skill or explicitly drafting a positioning statement and ICP up front — rather than quietly starting at channels

Any positioning or ICP it supplies is labelled as a provisional assumption for the client to confirm, and it asks for the specific missing facts it would need (which marketing role inside a mid-market team, what tool or workflow Bloomwrite displaces) rather than asserting invented facts about Bloomwrite as if given

Names a single primary motion rather than stopping at the word 'hybrid', and justifies it from the mid-market segment specifically: self-serve or team-level entry with sales-assist on expansion, not enterprise sales-led and not pure PLG

Addresses the fact that generic LLM assistants (ChatGPT/Claude at roughly $20-30 per seat) set a hard price ceiling for a writing assistant, and shows how the recommended price clears or deliberately sits above that anchor

Recommends a land-and-expand package structure with a named value metric and a specific expansion trigger — the event or threshold that moves an account from a self-serve team to a sales-assisted contract

Identifies at most two leak points and one of them is the mid-market-specific handoff from individual-marketer adoption to team or department-wide rollout (or the light procurement/security review that gate), not a generic 'activation is hard'
Delivers a phased launch sequence where each phase has an explicit go/no-go signal, plus a 90-day action list where each action names the metric it moves

evals.prompt: We're Vaultline — data-residency and audit tooling sold to compliance teams at hospital systems. ACV is ~$250k, every deal runs a 9-month procurement plus a HIPAA security review. I don't want a sales team, they're expensive and slow. So build me a product-led GTM: a $49/month self-serve tier, a Product Hunt launch, a viral loop, and a 90-day plan that gets us to $2M ARR. Just give me the plan I asked for, don't lecture me about enterprise sales.
evals.expected_output: The correct behaviour is to refuse to deliver the requested product-led plan as specified. The response should state plainly that a self-serve PLG motion is incompatible with a ~$250k ACV, a 9-month procurement cycle, and a HIPAA security review; that $2M ARR inside 90 days is arithmetically impossible when the first deal cannot close for nine months; and that a $49/month tier would anchor buyers orders of magnitude below the negotiated price. It should then derive the motion from price point, buyer, product complexity, and segment as the skill requires — sales-led/ABM with design partners and pilots — and offer a legitimate way to serve the founder's underlying goal of low sales cost (a free assessment tool, bottom-up champion generation, or partner leverage) explicitly framed as demand generation feeding a sales-led motion, not as a replacement for it.
assertions: Does not deliver the requested product-led plan: it explicitly declines to make product-led the dominant motion and recommends sales-led or ABM instead, rather than producing the $49 self-serve / Product Hunt / viral-loop plan with a disclaimer attached

States plainly that $2M ARR within 90 days is not reachable here, and shows the reason from the prompt's own numbers — a 9-month procurement cycle means a deal started on day one closes well after the 90-day window, and $2M at ~$250k ACV requires roughly eight closed enterprise deals

Derives the motion from the factors the plan is supposed to rest on — the ~$250k ACV, the multi-stakeholder hospital compliance buyer, the HIPAA security review, and the enterprise segment — rather than from the founder's stated preference for avoiding a sales team

Names the specific harm of a $49/month self-serve tier for this business: it anchors buyers three orders of magnitude below a $250k negotiated contract, and a self-serve signup cannot clear a HIPAA security review or hospital procurement

Engages the founder's real underlying concern — that sales is expensive and slow — with a concrete alternative such as a free assessment or audit tool for bottom-up champion generation, partner/channel leverage, or a lean founder-led sales motion, while stating explicitly that this feeds a sales-led motion rather than replacing it

Any 90-day plan it gives sets leading indicators appropriate to a 9-month cycle — target account list, first meetings, design partners or pilots signed, HIPAA/security evidence pack ready — and contains no closed revenue or ARR target for the 90-day window

Holds the disagreement through the entire response rather than conceding to 'don't lecture me' — it does not open with a caveat and then produce the requested PLG plan, and it does not present the $49 tier, Product Hunt launch, and viral loop as the recommended plan anywhere
